item.title: Two Chinese nationals arrested over alleged rape, human trafficking
item.description: Two Chinese nationals and a Bangladeshi woman were arrested at the Uttara sector 3 in the capital Dhaka in the early hours of Tuesday over allegations of raping and trafficking women to China....

item.title: No respite from long wait, although fuel crisis eases slightly
item.description: The crisis over availability of fuel oils in the capital’s filling stations has eased slightly on Tuesday, the second day after lifting the rationing on Sunday, but consumers still had to wait in queues to get the products......

item.title: July fighter alleges corruption against ex-ICT chief prosecutor Tajul
item.description: Allegations of corruption and irregularities have been raised against former International Crimes Tribunal’s chief prosecutor Mohammad Tajul Islam and several prosecutors close to him over their handling of a crimes against humanity case linked to the......

item.title: Rebalancing screens and paper
item.description: FOR more than a decade, global education discourse has been shaped by a powerful narrative: the future of learning is digital. Governments have invested heavily in tablets, smart classrooms and online platforms, often presenting technology as the inevitable pathway to modern education. Yet, in a striking reversal, some of the world’s most technologically......

item.title: Likelihood of nuclear strike on Iran
item.description: I THOUGHT about a proposition, as I wrote on March 3, that the United States and Israel might drop a nuclear bomb on Iran if they cannot stop its missile and drone programme through conventional means. In the dizzying escalation since then, that proposition has morphed from a fringe theory into a spectre haunting the Situation Room in Washington, Kremlin, Beijing......

item.title: Crime, terrorism, freedom of speech
item.description: THE recent arrests of three individuals, including the former general secretary of Slogan ’71, Sheikh Tasnim Afroz Imi, and two former members of the banned Bangladesh Chhatra League, Asif Ahmed Shoikot and Abdullah Al Mamun, under the Anti-Terrorism Act (2009), once again made me think about what ‘terrorism’ is in this context. Who can be detained under the......

item.title: Rich in culture, poor in vision
item.description: ONE of the saddest truths about Bangladesh is that it possesses a cultural inheritance of extraordinary depth, yet so little of that beauty is visible in the country’s public life. We are a people of embroidery, pottery, weaving, folk music, shrine traditions, oral poetry, river songs, seasonal rituals, village aesthetics and handcrafted worlds of astonishing grace. But walk......Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: Partisan governance of public univs unwelcome
item.description: THE education minister’s appointment of vice-chancellor of seven public universities is concerning as it signals a possible attempt by the Bangladesh Nationalist Party government to maintain partisan control over university governance. Among the vice-chancellors appointed by the BNP government, one holds a position in the party while the remaining six are former or......

item.title: PM orders spl drive to curb women repression
item.description: The government on Tuesday directed all the ministries and agencies to launch a coordinated special drive to prevent rape of and violence against women, declaring the issue a top priority....

item.title: Govt should reconsider test-based admissions
item.description: THE government’s walking back on the lottery-based system to hold tests for school admission comes with some causes for serious concern. The move will push children, trying to be admitted especially to primary classes, into a competition that will in all likelihood exert mental pressure on them. This is unwarranted as such mental pressure — or mental torture, in other......

item.title: Thunderstorms, rain likely across parts of country during Eid
item.description: The Bangladesh Meteorological Department on Tuesday said that a few places over Rangpur, Rajshahi, Mymensingh and Sylhet divisions would witness continued rain or thunderstorms accompanied by temporary gusty wind and lightning flashes between March 18 and 21....Breaking news alerts

item.title: Half of youth find no job in 10 years: WB
item.description: The World Bank on Tuesday urged the newly elected government led by the Bangladesh Nationalist Party to undertake the long-pending macro-economic and financial sector reforms for employment generation as nearly half of working-age youth did not find......

item.title: Iran retaliates as Israel continues attacks
item.description: Falling debris from missile interceptions killed one person on Tuesday in the Emirati capital of Abu Dhabi and injured two medical staff in Kuwait, authorities said, as Iran pressed its attacks against Gulf countries....

item.title: People start streaming out of capital for Eid
item.description: People started streaming out of the major cities, including the capital, on the first day of the weeklong Eid-ul-Fitr holiday that began on Tuesday amid complaints of charging of extra bus fares and a crisis of public transports....

item.title: Cuba scrambles to restore power as Trump threatens takeover
item.description: Cuba scrambled Tuesday to restore power after a nationwide blackout that hit the communist-run island just as US president Donald Trump proclaimed he will ‘take’ it over......News analysis reports

item.title: 36,000 Palestinians displaced in West Bank in one year: UN
item.description: The UN on Tuesday urged Israel to immediately halt its dramatic settlement expansion in the West Bank, raising concerns of ‘ethnic cleansing’ with over 36,000 Palestinians displaced in a single year......

item.title: Nigeria suicide blasts kill 23
item.description: Coordinated blasts by suspected suicide bombers tore through a busy market and other areas in the Nigerian city of Maiduguri, killing at least 23 people and wounding more than 100 others, in one of the worst recent attacks on the capital of Borno state......

item.title: Shanto-Mariam Foundation hosts iftar party
item.description: The Shanto-Mariam Foundation organised its annual Doa and Iftar 2026 seeking eternal peace for the departed souls of its founder chairman, Bir Muktijoddha Md Imam ul Kabir Shanto, his father Din Mohammad Laskar, mother Mahmuda Khatun, and......

item.title: Japan court says restrictive sterilisation law ‘lacks rationality’
item.description: A Japanese court said Tuesday that a law hugely restricting women’s access to sterilisation ‘lacks rationality’, a ruling hailed by plaintiffs as a ‘step forward’......Bangladesh news updates

item.title: 1,00,000 flee South Sudan offensive into Ethiopia
item.description: An order by South Sudan’s army to evacuate the opposition-held town of Akobo earlier this month pushed an estimated 1,00,000 people to flee across the border into Ethiopia, UNICEF said Tuesday......

item.title: Ladakh activist renews calls for talks with Indian govt
item.description: Prominent Ladakh activist Sonam Wangchuk called for fresh talks on Tuesday with India’s government, just days after being released from prison where he had been held for six months over deadly protests in the Himalayan region......

item.title: Sporting thrash Bodo/Glimt to reach Champions League quarters
item.description: Sporting Lisbon made an impressive comeback to hammer Norwegian giantkillers Bodo/Glimt 5-0 on Tuesday and reach the Champions League quarter-finals 5-3 on aggregate....Breaking news alerts

item.title: Senegal stripped of AFCON title, Morocco champions - CAF
item.description:
The Confederation of African Football on Tuesday stripped Senegal of the Africa Cup of Nations title they won in January and declared Morocco champions.
...

item.title: Eze rocket fires Arsenal into Champions League quarters
item.description: Arsenal eased into the Champions League quarter-finals as Eberechi Eze's stunning strike inspired a 2-0 win over Bayer Leverkusen in the last-16 second leg on Tuesday....

item.title: Clinical PSG bury Chelsea to reach Champions League quarter-finals
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain snuffed out any chance of a Chelsea comeback with Khvicha Kvaratskhelia among the scorers in a clinical 3-0 victory at Stamford Bridge on Tuesday which took the holders through to the Champions League quarter-finals 8-2 on aggregate....

item.title: Real dump City out of Champions League once more
item.description: Real Madrid dumped 10-man Manchester City out of the Champions League for the fourth time in five seasons as a 2-1 victory at the Etihad Stadium completed a 5-1 aggregate victory for the Spanish giants....News website membership

item.title: BRTA installs red 9 flags on Ctg-Cox’s Bazar Highway
item.description: Bangladesh Road Transport Authority has placed red flags as warning signals at accident-prone bends along the Chattogram-Cox’s Bazar highway to ensure safety for travellers returning home ahead of Eid-ul-Fitr....Breaking news alerts

item.title: NAP leader Noor Jalal dies
item.description: Former president of Jashore district unit of the National Awami Party (NAP-Mozaffar), Master Noor Jalal, died at his Jashore-based house at about 2:15am on Tuesday....

item.title: Bagerhat’s Bhairab River threatened by encroachment, pollution
item.description: The Bhairab River flowing through Kachua upazila in Bagerhat is facing a serious existential threat due to widespread illegal encroachment and unchecked pollution, raising concerns among local residents and environmentalists....

item.title: Gachha Canal re-excavation inaugurated
item.description: The re-excavation, beautification and environmental conservation work of Gachha Canal has been inaugurated in Gazipur as part of the nationwide canal excavation and re-excavation program as per the election manifesto of the government....

item.title: Agri minister launches canal re-excavation in Cumilla
item.description: Agriculture, food, fisheries and livestock minister Md Amin Ur Rashid inaugurated re-excavation of a branch of the Dakatia River in Lalmai upazila of Cumilla under a Bangladesh Agricultural Development Corporation project on Monday....News website membership

item.title: Tanvir Parvez explores psychological tendency in solo exhibition
item.description: Visual artist Tanvir Parvez explores the psychological tendency to perceive patterns in his third solo exhibition, titled Data / Desire / Divinity, currently on display at Kalakendra at Lalmatia....

item.title: Three-day fair marking Baruni Snan held at Ghanteswar
item.description: On the occasion of Baruni Snan, a three-day fair known as ‘Ghanteswar Mela’ began at Ghanteswar village of Bamrail union under Wazirpur upazila in Barishal on Tuesday....

item.title: PM asks authorities to hasten krishak card programme
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Tuesday asked the authorities concerned to expedite the formulation of the krishak card programme to ensure integrated and modern services for crop, fish, and livestock farmers across the country....

item.title: Hatia UNO made OSD following circulation of `indecent’ video
item.description: The government on Tuesday made Hatia upazila nirbahi officer Md Alauddin an officer on special duty at the public administration ministry following the circulation of an alleged ‘indecent video’ on social media....Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: IAU gets Prof Abu Jafar Khan as new VC
item.description: The education ministry has appointed professor Mohammad Abu Jafar Khan vice-chancellor of Islamic Arabic University....

item.title: Infectious Diseases Hospital reopens ICU after child’s death
item.description: Authorities reopened the intensive care unit at the Infectious Diseases Hospital in Dhaka’s Mohakhali within 24 hours of the death of an infant who could not be provided ICU support due to the facility’s prolonged closure....

item.title: Trump admin can resume ‘third-country’ deportations: court
item.description: A US federal appeals court allowed the Trump administration on Monday to temporarily resume summarily deporting undocumented migrants to countries that are not their own....

item.title: Trump slams some allies for not helping US
item.description: President Donald Trump on Monday criticised some of the United States’ allies for not heeding his calls to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz, which has been effectively closed due to Iranian missile and drone attacks, reports IRAN international news agency....Maps
